audit_status.source_base: Hebrew 2 Samuel chapter 22, first-pass poetic draft aligned later; compare Psalm 18
audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for royal victory-song genre, Divine-warrior imagery, cosmic storm language, Sheol/death metaphors, righteousness and blamelessness claims, violence training, enemy destruction, foreign submission, imperial rule, covenant language, and tension with David's documented abuses.

verses.restored: David spoke to YHWH words of this song on day YHWH rescued him from palm of all his enemies and from palm of Saul.
He said: YHWH is my rock-crag, my fortress, and my deliverer.
Divine is my rock in whom I take refuge, my shield and horn of my rescue, my stronghold and refuge, my savior; from violence you save me.
I call upon YHWH, worthy of praise, and I am rescued from my enemies.
For waves of death surrounded me; torrents of destruction/Belial terrified me.
Cords of Sheol surrounded me; snares of death confronted me.
In my distress I called upon YHWH; to my Divine I called. From temple YHWH heard my voice, and my cry entered YHWH's ears.
Then earth shook and quaked; foundations of heavens trembled and shook because YHWH was angry.
Smoke rose from YHWH's nostrils, and fire from YHWH's mouth devoured; coals burned from it.
YHWH bowed heavens and came down, with thick darkness beneath YHWH's feet.
YHWH rode upon a cherub and flew, appearing upon wings of wind.
YHWH made darkness around as shelter: gatherings of waters, thick clouds of skies.
From brightness before YHWH, coals of fire burned.
YHWH thundered from heavens; Most High gave voice.
YHWH sent arrows and scattered them, lightning and threw them into confusion.
Channels of sea appeared; foundations of world were uncovered at rebuke of YHWH, from breath of wind of YHWH's nostrils.
YHWH sent from height and took me, drawing me from many waters.
YHWH rescued me from my strong enemy, from those who hated me, because they were stronger than I.
They confronted me in day of my calamity, but YHWH was my support.
YHWH brought me out to a broad place; YHWH rescued me because YHWH delighted in me.
YHWH dealt with me according to my righteousness; according to cleanness of my hands YHWH returned to me.
For I kept ways of YHWH and did not wickedly turn from my Divine.
For all YHWH's judgments were before me, and statutes I did not turn from.
I was whole/blameless toward YHWH and guarded myself from my iniquity.
YHWH returned to me according to my righteousness, according to my cleanness before YHWH's eyes.
With one of covenant-loyalty you show covenant-loyalty; with a whole/blameless warrior you show yourself whole.
With one purified you show yourself pure; with one twisted you show yourself astute/contending.
You rescue afflicted people, but your eyes are against haughty ones; you bring them low.
For you are my lamp, YHWH; YHWH illuminates my darkness.
For by you I run against a troop; by my Divine I leap over a wall.
As for Divine, the way is complete; word of YHWH is refined. YHWH is a shield to all who take refuge.
For who is Divine except YHWH, and who is a rock except our Divine?
Divine is my strong refuge and makes my way complete.
YHWH makes my feet like deer and causes me to stand upon my high places.
YHWH trains my hands for battle, so my arms bend a bow of bronze.
You gave me shield of your rescue, and your answering/gentleness made me great.
You broadened my step beneath me, and my ankles did not slip.
I pursued my enemies and destroyed them; I did not return until I consumed them.
I consumed them and crushed them so they did not rise; they fell beneath my feet.
You girded me with strength for battle; you caused those rising against me to bow beneath me.
You gave me backs/necks of my enemies, and those hating me I destroyed.
They looked about, but there was no savior; to YHWH, but YHWH did not answer them.
I ground them like dust of earth; like mud of streets I crushed and stamped them.
You rescued me from disputes of my people; you kept me as head of nations. A people I did not know serves me.
Foreigners submit/come cringing to me; at hearing of ear they listen to me.
Foreigners fade and come trembling from their fortresses.
YHWH lives. Blessed is my rock; exalted is Divine, rock of my rescue.
The Divine who gives me vengeance and brings peoples down beneath me,
who brings me out from my enemies. You raise me above those rising against me; from man of violence you rescue me.
Therefore I will thank you, YHWH, among nations, and to your name I will make music.
YHWH is a tower of rescue for YHWH's king and does covenant-kindness for YHWH's anointed, for David and his seed forever.

translation_notes: This is a royal victory song and theological self-portrait, not a neutral biography. Its claims of clean hands and blameless conduct stand in sharp tension with Bathsheba, Uriah, Joab's impunity, the concubines, imperial executions, and other harms narrated around it.

The cosmic descent, smoke, fire, thunder, sea exposure, arrows, and lightning are ancient storm-theophany poetry. They are not scientific description or permission to attribute disasters to Divine rage.
Death, Sheol, waves, and snares describe extremity and rescue; they should not be used to shame fear, trauma, depression, or suicidal crisis.
Verses 21-25 can express a particular experience of deliverance without establishing that safety proves righteousness or suffering proves guilt.
'Blameless' and 'clean hands' must not become leader immunity, denial of survivor testimony, or a doctrine that an anointed ruler cannot abuse.

Training hands for war, pursuing, consuming, grinding, and trampling enemies are violent royal imagery. They may not be recruited into modern holy war, vigilantism, militarized masculinity, or spiritual warfare against human beings.
Verse 42 portrays enemies receiving no answer. Divine silence cannot be inferred from defeat, ethnicity, political opposition, illness, or unanswered prayer.
Foreign submission and rule over unknown peoples reflect imperial ideology. Cringing subjects are not evidence of benevolent governance.

The covenant ending belongs within Jewish scripture. It may not justify hereditary autocracy, Christian supersessionism, modern territorial entitlement, or exemption of Davidic leaders from accountability.
safety_note: Read this song beside the narrative record, especially the people harmed by David's power. Do not turn poetic claims of righteousness into factual acquittal, violent imagery into present-day instruction, or national victory into proof that Divine values one people above another. Refuge in YHWH must remain compatible with truth, survivor testimony, and accountability.
